description: A comprehensive support ticket management system that handles user authentication, ticket lifecycle operations, commenting, and analytics for customer support workflows.

tools.name: login
tools.description: Authenticate a user with credentials and establish an active session for accessing ticket system operations.

username.description: The username credential for authenticating the user account

password.description: The password credential for authenticating the user account

success.description: Indicates whether the authentication attempt was successful

session_token.description: The session token issued upon successful authentication, used for subsequent authenticated requests

username.description: The username of the successfully authenticated user account

tools.name: logout
tools.description: Terminate the current user session and invalidate the authentication token.

success.description: Indicates whether the logout operation completed successfully

tools.name: check_login_status
tools.description: Verify the current authentication state and retrieve active session information.

is_authenticated.description: Indicates whether a valid user session is currently active

username.description: The username of the currently authenticated user account, if a session is active

tools.name: create_ticket
tools.description: Create a new support ticket with specified details, priority, and optional assignment. Requires an authenticated session.

title.description: The title or subject line summarizing the ticket issue or request

description.description: Detailed description of the issue, request, or context for the ticket. Defaults to empty string if not provided.

priority.description: Priority level of the ticket ranging from 1 (lowest) to 5 (highest priority). Defaults to 1 if not specified.

category.description: The category classifying the type of ticket. Valid values are: technical, billing, general, feature_request, or bug_report. Defaults to 'general' if not provided.

assignee.description: The username of the staff member to assign this ticket to. If not provided, the ticket remains unassigned.

ticket_id.description: The unique identifier assigned to the newly created ticket

title.description: The title or subject line of the created ticket

description.description: The detailed description of the ticket issue or request

status.description: The current status of the ticket, which is 'open' for newly created tickets

priority.description: The priority level assigned to the ticket, ranging from 1 (lowest) to 5 (highest)

category.description: The category classifying the type of ticket

created_by.description: The username of the authenticated user who created the ticket

created_at.description: The timestamp when the ticket was created, in YYYY-MM-DD HH:MM:SS format

assignee.description: The username of the staff member assigned to the ticket, if an assignee was specified

tools.name: get_ticket
tools.description: Retrieve comprehensive details about a specific ticket using its unique identifier.

ticket_id.description: The unique identifier of the ticket to retrieve
The unique identifier of the ticket

title.description: The title or subject line of the ticket

description.description: The detailed description of the ticket issue or request

status.description: The current status of the ticket in its lifecycle. Valid values are: open, in_progress, resolved, or closed.

priority.description: The priority level assigned to the ticket, ranging from 1 (lowest) to 5 (highest)

category.description: The category classifying the type of ticket

created_by.description: The username of the user who created the ticket

created_at.description: The timestamp when the ticket was created, in YYYY-MM-DD HH:MM:SS format

updated_at.description: The timestamp of the most recent update to the ticket, in YYYY-MM-DD HH:MM:SS format

assignee.description: The username of the staff member assigned to the ticket, if assigned

resolution.description: Detailed description of how the ticket was resolved or the solution provided, if the ticket has been resolved

tools.name: list_tickets
tools.description: Retrieve a paginated list of tickets with optional filtering by status, priority, category, or assignee. Requires an authenticated session.

status.description: Filter tickets by their current status. Valid values are: open, in_progress, resolved, or closed. If not provided, returns tickets of all statuses.

priority.description: Filter tickets by priority level, ranging from 1 (lowest) to 5 (highest). If not provided, returns tickets of all priority levels.

category.description: Filter tickets by category. Valid values are: technical, billing, general, feature_request, or bug_report. If not provided, returns tickets of all categories.

assignee.description: Filter tickets by the username of the assigned staff member. If not provided, returns tickets for all assignees including unassigned tickets.

limit.description: Maximum number of tickets to return in the result set. Defaults to 5 if not specified.

offset.description: Number of tickets to skip before returning results, used for pagination. Defaults to 0 if not specified.

tickets.description: A list of ticket summaries matching the specified filters

ticket_id.description: The unique identifier of the ticket, which can be used with get_ticket to retrieve full details

title.description: The title or subject line of the ticket

status.description: The current status of the ticket in its lifecycle

priority.description: The priority level assigned to the ticket, ranging from 1 (lowest) to 5 (highest)

category.description: The category classifying the type of ticket

created_by.description: The username of the user who created the ticket

created_at.description: The timestamp when the ticket was created, in YYYY-MM-DD HH:MM:SS format

assignee.description: The username of the staff member assigned to the ticket, if assigned

tools.name: search_tickets
tools.description: Search for tickets by matching keywords in their title or description fields. Requires an authenticated session.

query.description: The search keyword or phrase to match against ticket titles and descriptions

status.description: Filter search results by ticket status. Valid values are: open, in_progress, resolved, or closed. If not provided, searches across all statuses.

limit.description: Maximum number of matching tickets to return in the result set. Defaults to 5 if not specified.

tickets.description: A list of tickets matching the search query

ticket_id.description: The unique identifier of the ticket, which can be used with get_ticket to retrieve full details

title.description: The title or subject line of the ticket

description.description: The detailed description of the ticket issue or request

status.description: The current status of the ticket in its lifecycle

priority.description: The priority level assigned to the ticket, ranging from 1 (lowest) to 5 (highest)

created_by.description: The username of the user who created the ticket

created_at.description: The timestamp when the ticket was created, in YYYY-MM-DD HH:MM:SS format

tools.name: update_ticket
tools.description: Modify one or more fields of an existing ticket with partial update support. Requires an authenticated session.

ticket_id.description: The unique identifier of the ticket to update

title.description: New title or subject line for the ticket

description.description: New detailed description for the ticket

priority.description: New priority level for the ticket, ranging from 1 (lowest) to 5 (highest)

category.description: New category for the ticket. Valid values are: technical, billing, general, feature_request, or bug_report.

assignee.description: New assignee username for the ticket. Provide an empty string to remove the current assignee.

status.description: New status for the ticket. Valid values are: open, in_progress, resolved, or closed.

ticket_id.description: The unique identifier of the updated ticket

status.description: Confirmation message indicating the update operation was successful

updated_fields.description: List of field names that were modified during the update operation

updated_at.description: The timestamp when the ticket was updated, in YYYY-MM-DD HH:MM:SS format

tools.name: close_ticket
tools.description: Mark a ticket as closed without providing resolution details. Requires an authenticated session.

ticket_id.description: The unique identifier of the ticket to close
The unique identifier of the closed ticket

status.description: The updated status of the ticket, which is 'closed'

message.description: Confirmation message indicating the ticket was successfully closed

updated_at.description: The timestamp when the ticket was closed, in YYYY-MM-DD HH:MM:SS format

tools.name: resolve_ticket
tools.description: Mark a ticket as resolved and record the resolution details explaining how the issue was addressed. Requires an authenticated session.

ticket_id.description: The unique identifier of the ticket to resolve

resolution.description: Detailed description of how the ticket was resolved or the solution provided to address the issue

ticket_id.description: The unique identifier of the resolved ticket

status.description: The updated status of the ticket, which is 'resolved'

resolution.description: The detailed description of how the ticket was resolved or the solution provided

resolved_at.description: The timestamp when the ticket was marked as resolved, in YYYY-MM-DD HH:MM:SS format

updated_at.description: The timestamp when the ticket was updated, in YYYY-MM-DD HH:MM:SS format

tools.name: get_user_tickets
tools.description: Retrieve all tickets created by a specific user or the currently authenticated user. Requires an authenticated session.

username.description: The username to filter tickets by. If not provided, returns tickets created by the currently authenticated user.

status.description: Filter tickets by their current status. Valid values are: open, in_progress, resolved, or closed. If not provided, returns tickets of all statuses.

limit.description: Maximum number of tickets to return in the result set. Defaults to 5 if not specified.

tickets.description: A list of tickets created by the specified user

ticket_id.description: The unique identifier of the ticket, which can be used with get_ticket to retrieve full details

title.description: The title or subject line of the ticket

status.description: The current status of the ticket in its lifecycle

priority.description: The priority level assigned to the ticket, ranging from 1 (lowest) to 5 (highest)

category.description: The category classifying the type of ticket

created_at.description: The timestamp when the ticket was created, in YYYY-MM-DD HH:MM:SS format

assignee.description: The username of the staff member assigned to the ticket, if assigned

tools.name: add_comment
tools.description: Add a comment to a ticket, with support for public or internal (staff-only) visibility. Requires an authenticated session.

ticket_id.description: The unique identifier of the ticket to add a comment to

comment.description: The text content of the comment to add to the ticket

is_internal.description: If true, the comment is marked as internal and visible only to staff members. If false, the comment is public. Defaults to false.

comment_id.description: The unique identifier assigned to the newly created comment

ticket_id.description: The unique identifier of the ticket the comment was added to

comment.description: The text content of the comment

author.description: The username of the authenticated user who authored the comment

is_internal.description: Indicates whether the comment is internal (staff-only) or public

created_at.description: The timestamp when the comment was created, in YYYY-MM-DD HH:MM:SS format

tools.name: get_ticket_comments
tools.description: Retrieve all comments associated with a specific ticket, with optional inclusion of internal staff-only comments. Requires an authenticated session.

ticket_id.description: The unique identifier of the ticket to retrieve comments for

include_internal.description: If true, includes internal staff-only comments in the results (requires staff privileges). If false, returns only public comments. Defaults to false.

comments.description: A list of comments associated with the ticket

comment_id.description: The unique identifier of the comment

ticket_id.description: The unique identifier of the ticket the comment belongs to

comment.description: The text content of the comment

author.description: The username of the user who authored the comment

is_internal.description: Indicates whether the comment is internal (staff-only) or public

created_at.description: The timestamp when the comment was created, in YYYY-MM-DD HH:MM:SS format

tools.name: get_ticket_statistics
tools.description: Retrieve aggregated statistics and metrics about tickets in the system for a specified time period. Requires an authenticated session.

time_period.description: The time period to calculate statistics for. Valid values are: today, week, month, year, or all. Defaults to 'all' if not specified.

total_tickets.description: The total count of tickets in the system for the specified time period

open_tickets.description: The count of tickets with 'open' status

in_progress_tickets.description: The count of tickets with 'in_progress' status

resolved_tickets.description: The count of tickets with 'resolved' status

closed_tickets.description: The count of tickets with 'closed' status

average_resolution_time.description: The average time in hours taken to resolve tickets during the specified time period

tickets_by_category.description: A breakdown of ticket counts grouped by category (technical, billing, general, feature_request, bug_report)

tickets_by_priority.description: A breakdown of ticket counts grouped by priority level (1 through 5)
